daily_brief: Hopes for an end to the US-Israel war with Iran dominated markets and diplomacy today, with oil prices falling sharply as Secretary of State Marco Rubio indicated a deal could be announced imminently [BBC, Financial Times]. However, Iran's foreign ministry cautioned that an agreement 'is not imminent,' and President Trump urged negotiators not to rush [BBC, Le Monde]. The potential reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, closed since February, would significantly ease global energy pressures. Meanwhile, Russia launched one of its heaviest attacks on Ukraine since the invasion began, firing hundreds of drones and the nuclear-capable Oreshnik hypersonic missile at Kyiv, killing four and injuring approximately 100 [BBC, RFI]. In Turkey, riot police stormed the headquarters of the main opposition CHP party following a court ruling that ousted its leadership, deepening concerns about democratic erosion under President Erdoğan [BBC, France 24]. The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo continues to escalate rapidly, with suspected cases now exceeding 900 and the WHO warning of severe impediments to containment due to ongoing conflict in Ituri province [Guardian, RFI, RNZ]. Asian markets rallied on Iran deal optimism, with Japan's Topix reaching an all-time high [Nikkei Asia].

top_stories.headline: US-Iran Peace Deal Talks Intensify as Oil Prices Slide, but Tehran Cautions Agreement Not Imminent
bullets: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated from New Delhi that negotiators have a 'pretty solid thing on the table' and an agreement could be reached Monday, while President Trump said he instructed negotiators 'not to rush into' a deal

Iran's foreign ministry confirmed progress on 'a large portion of the issues' but stated no one can claim an agreement is imminent; reports indicate Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's location is undisclosed, complicating communications
The proposed memorandum of understanding reportedly includes a 60-day ceasefire extension, reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and further negotiations on Iran's nuclear program
Brent crude fell 5.5% to $97.90/barrel and US crude dropped 5.9% to $90.93 on Monday morning; Asian stock markets rose on the news

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Detailed coverage of both US and Iranian positions, noted intelligence reports about Khamenei's location

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Focused on oil price movements and market reaction

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Rubio's warning that US would find 'another way' if talks fail

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Iran's position that negotiations concern ending war, not nuclear issues

sources.name: Nikkei Asia
sources.framing_note: Focused on market impacts including Japan stocks hitting all-time high

top_stories.headline: Russia Launches Massive Missile and Drone Barrage on Kyiv, Deploys Nuclear-Capable Oreshnik
bullets: Russia fired hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles at Ukraine Saturday night, with Kyiv as the main target; four killed and approximately 100 injured
Russia's defense ministry confirmed use of the Oreshnik hypersonic missile, capable of carrying nuclear warheads, marking only the third use of this weapon system in the war
Damage reported at approximately 50 locations including residential buildings, schools, an opera house, and a museum across Kyiv
EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas called the Oreshnik use 'reckless nuclear brinkmanship' and announced EU diplomats would meet to discuss increasing pressure on Russia

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Balanced coverage including Russia's claim the strikes responded to Ukrainian attacks on civilian infrastructure

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Additional coverage of Luhansk dormitory strike that Russia claims killed 21, which Ukraine denies targeting

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized European condemnation and nuclear implications of Oreshnik use

top_stories.headline: Turkish Riot Police Storm Opposition Party Headquarters After Court Ousts Leadership
bullets: Turkish riot police used tear gas to force entry into Republican People's Party (CHP) headquarters in Ankara on Sunday after party members barricaded entrances
An appeals court ruling Thursday declared party leader Özgür Özel's election null and void, ordering replacement by Kemal Kilicdaroğlu, who lost to Erdoğan in 2023 presidential election
Ankara's governor ordered police to 'implement the court decision' after Kilicdaroğlu's representatives requested 'necessary procedures' to take control of headquarters
Özel declared 'We are under attack' via social media as security forces entered; clashes reported between supporters of both leaders

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Described move as 'further cementing President Erdoğan's grip on power'

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Brief video coverage characterizing situation as 'political crisis'

top_stories.headline: DRC Ebola Outbreak Surpasses 900 Suspected Cases as Violence Impedes Response
bullets: WHO Director-General confirmed more than 900 suspected Ebola cases including 101 confirmed cases; DRC health ministry reported 204 deaths
Outbreak centered in Ituri province where nearly 5 million people live amid ongoing conflict; 1 in 4 need humanitarian assistance and 1 in 5 are internally displaced
Arson attacks destroyed two Ebola treatment centers last week, reflecting community distrust and anger in conflict-affected region
The outbreak involves the rarer Bundibugyo strain for which no approved vaccines or treatments exist; contact tracing and isolation are only proven containment methods

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Emphasized attacks on health workers and impact of aid cuts on vulnerable communities

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Focused on WHO statements and surveillance scaling efforts

sources.name: RNZ
sources.framing_note: Highlighted community fear, distrust, and the burning of health centers

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Questioned how the rare strain 'outran doctors'

top_stories.headline: NATO Allies Seek Clarity as US Announces Contradictory Troop Deployment Decisions
bullets: President Trump announced 5,000 additional troops for Poland one week after cancelling a planned 4,000-troop deployment to the same country
Earlier this month, the US announced troops would be pulled out of Germany; some US forces are currently deployed to the Middle East for the Iran conflict
Secretary of State Rubio told NATO foreign ministers in Sweden that the US constantly reevaluates troop presence based on global commitments
Trump said the Poland decision was based on his relationship with Polish President Karol Nawrocki, whom he backed in last year's election

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Noted the announcements 'have caused confusion among allies' of the trans-Atlantic defense organization

additional_stories.headline: Israel military issues evacuation warnings to 10 Lebanon villages citing Hezbollah ceasefire violations
Toyota to cut overseas production by 83,000 vehicles through November due to prolonged Strait of Hormuz blockade
Trump administration pauses deportations to DRC over Ebola but strands woman in Kinshasa despite judge's return order
Pacific Ocean temperatures rising fast, raising 80% chance of El Niño by July with potential for rare 'super' event
SpaceX prepares for largest IPO in history as Musk's rocket company plans Nasdaq listing
Messi substituted with apparent hamstring issue three weeks before Argentina's World Cup title defense begins
Chemical tank heating up in California evacuation zone as 40,000 residents remain displaced
Senegalese president fires longtime ally Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko, triggering political instability
Myanmar military launches renewed offensives to reclaim rare earth mining regions and border trade routes
Around 20 injured after man sprays unknown substance at Tokyo luxury mall ATM
